success_surface: Cell-level formula insertion with dynamic range detection
effective_behaviors: Used dynamic range detection based on actual data end row rather than hardcoded ranges
Implemented header detection logic to adjust data start row appropriately
Wrote Excel formulas as raw strings using openpyxl for proper formula preservation
Targeted the exact answer position specified in task instructions (cell D2)
Saved output to new file path to preserve source file integrity
Added verification logging to confirm formula was correctly inserted before completion
Selected openpyxl library for formula manipulation rather than pandas
evidence: Code used ws.max_row to determine actual data end row dynamically
Logic checked ws.cell(row=1, column=1).value to detect header presence
Formula assigned directly to ws['D2'] matching answer_position
wb.save(output_path) used separate output file path
Print statements verified D2 Value and Calculated Result after assignment

memory_items.title: Dynamic Range Detection
memory_items.lesson: Always determine data ranges programmatically using max_row/max_column rather than assuming fixed boundaries
memory_items.generalization: When inserting formulas or operations spanning multiple rows/columns, derive the range dynamically from the workbook state to accommodate variable data sizes

memory_items.title: Header-Aware Row Positioning
memory_items.lesson: Check for header existence in row 1 and adjust data start row accordingly
memory_items.generalization: Before defining working ranges, inspect the first row for header markers to ensure data processing starts at the correct position

memory_items.title: Formula String Syntax
memory_items.lesson: Write Excel formulas as raw strings prefixed with '=' when using openpyxl
memory_items.generalization: For formula cells, use string format '=FORMULA()' exactly as it would appear in Excel rather than computing values in Python

memory_items.title: Output File Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Save modified workbooks to a new path to maintain source file integrity
memory_items.generalization: Always direct output to a separate filename/path unless in-place modification is explicitly required

memory_items.title: Pre-Completion Verification
memory_items.lesson: Log verification details about the manipulated cells before finishing execution
memory_items.generalization: After applying changes, confirm and report key properties like cell values, formula content, and expected results

edits.content:
## 6. Dynamic Range & Formula Patterns
- **Variable Data Sizes**: Derive formula ranges from max_row/max_column instead of hardcoded values; use f-string formatting to build range references dynamically.
- **Header Detection**: Before setting start_row, check row 1 for known header text to determine whether data begins at row 1 or row 2.
- **Formula Writing**: For formula cells, write raw Excel formula strings (e.g., '=SUMIF(A:A,B,BB)') via cell assignment; do not pre-compute values in Python unless requested.
- **Verification Logging**: After formula insertion, log the formula string and target cell to confirm correct placement before saving.
